topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The glow of the laptop screen illuminated Leo’s face, casting deep shadows under his eyes. He watched his daughter, Maya, scroll through an unfamiliar feed, a strange, almost guarded expression settling on her features. It was a private world, hidden behind a carefully constructed wall of teenage nonchalance. Leo felt a familiar, heavy ache in his chest, a tightening sensation that made breathing shallow. He wanted to reach out, to pull her back into the shared space of their home, but the silence between them felt vast and unbridgeable. He just sat there, his hands clenched tightly around the lukewarm mug of tea, the weight of this hidden existence pressing down on him like a physical burden.

[story 2]

"What is this?" The words tumbled out of Sarah's mouth, laced with a brittle edge that made her own voice sound unfamiliar. She found the login details tucked carelessly beneath a textbook. Seeing the curated perfection of Maya’s secret life—the filtered smiles, the carefully chosen aesthetics—sent a wave of profound disappointment washing over her. It wasn't anger that surfaced, but a deep, hollow weariness. Sarah pushed the laptop away slowly, the click of the lid echoing loudly in the quiet room. She walked to the window, staring out at the mundane street, feeling utterly adrift, as if she had missed some crucial, unspoken instruction manual for her own child.

[story 3]

I found the password written on a sticky note near the charging station. My stomach twisted into a tight knot. It wasn't malicious, exactly; it was just… a boundary crossed, a space carved out where I wasn't invited. Watching my kid inhabit this parallel reality, this carefully edited version of herself, made my throat feel dry. I stood by the door, my feet rooted to the spot, unable to move toward confrontation. Every instinct screamed to intervene, to smooth over the jagged edges of this secret, but I just stood, letting the silence stretch out, a heavy, suffocating blanket around my shoulders.
